title: JIT IR translation — closure operations
purpose: Closures (anonymous functions) are fundamental to Gleam's functional programming model — map, filter, fold, and higher-order functions all use closures. make_fun2 captures free variables into a heap-allocated closure object. apply dispatches a closure call. JIT-compiling these avoids interpreter overhead for closure creation and dispatch in hot loops (e.g. lists:map(fun, List) where the fun is called per element).
task: Implement IR translation for closure opcodes in crates/beamr/src/jit/ir_closure.rs. make_fun2 compiles to a heap allocation (safepoint + runtime helper) that captures free variables from the current stack frame. apply compiles to an indirect call through the closure's function pointer, with the captured variables loaded from the closure object.

requirements.title: Closure creation IR — make_fun2
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL translate make_fun2 into Cranelift IR that: (1) emits a GC safepoint with all free variables in the root set, (2) calls jit_alloc_closure(process, fun_entry, arity, num_free) to allocate a closure object on the process heap, (3) writes each free variable to the closure object at the correct offset. THE SYSTEM SHALL tag the resulting pointer as a boxed Term (fun tag).
acceptance: translate_make_fun2(builder, emitter, fun_entry, arity, free_vars, dest) emits safepoint + alloc + writes
jit_alloc_closure(process, fun_entry, arity, num_free) runtime helper allocates header + free var slots
Free variables are written to closure_ptr + header_size + (i * word_size)
Result is a tagged boxed Term with fun tag
Test: create closure capturing 2 free vars -> closure object has correct fun entry and captured values
Test: create closure capturing 0 free vars -> valid zero-capture closure

requirements.title: Closure dispatch IR — apply and call_fun
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL translate apply and call_fun opcodes into Cranelift IR that: (1) extracts the function entry pointer from the closure object, (2) loads captured free variables into the appropriate registers (appended after explicit arguments), (3) checks the JitCache for a compiled version of the closure's target function, (4) dispatches to native code (cache hit) or interpreter (cache miss). THE SYSTEM SHALL verify arity matches at the call site — arity mismatch raises badarity error.
acceptance: translate_apply(builder, emitter, fun_term, args, dest) emits closure unpack + dispatch
translate_call_fun(builder, emitter, fun_term, arity, dest) emits closure unpack + dispatch
Free variables from closure are loaded and passed as additional arguments after explicit args
Arity mismatch (explicit args != closure arity) raises badarity — free vars are captured in the closure object, not part of call-site arity
Cache hit: direct native call; cache miss: jit_call_interpreted with closure args
Test: create closure capturing x, apply it -> x accessible in closure body
Test: apply closure with wrong arity -> badarity error

requirements.title: Closure as GC root
requirements.spec: WHEN a closure Term is live across a GC safepoint, THE SYSTEM SHALL track it in the SafepointEmitter root set. THE SYSTEM SHALL ensure the GC can relocate the closure object and update all references (the closure contains boxed free variables that may themselves be relocatable). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT special-case closure root tracking — closures are boxed Terms like tuples and follow the same GC root protocol.
acceptance: Closure Terms are tracked via SafepointEmitter::track_root like any boxed Term
GC can relocate closure objects during safepoint collection
Free variables inside closures are reachable from the closure root (GC follows the boxed header)
Test: create closure, trigger GC, apply closure -> free variables still correct after relocation

requirements.title: Opcode dispatch wiring for closure opcodes
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL update the main opcode dispatch in compiler.rs to route make_fun2, apply, and call_fun opcodes to ir_closure.rs translations instead of emitting deopt exits. THE SYSTEM SHALL leave fun_info and is_function2 opcodes as deopt.
acceptance: make_fun2, apply, call_fun opcodes route to ir_closure.rs translations
fun_info and is_function2 still deopt to interpreter
Test: function that creates and calls a closure compiles without deopt

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement closure inlining — each closure call is a full dispatch
SHALL NOT implement closure specialisation (monomorphisation for known closure bodies) — deferred to future optimisation
SHALL NOT implement fun_info introspection from compiled code — deoptimise to interpreter
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
ir_closure.rs is under 500 lines of production code
Closure creation emits GC safepoint before allocation
Closure dispatch correctly loads free variables and checks arity
